days.title: Report Repair
days.essence: Find the two (then three) numbers that sum to 2020 and multiply them. A nested-loop subset-sum warmup whose only point is learning the input/IO format.

days.title: Password Philosophy
days.essence: Parse each line into a policy (min-max + character) and password, then validate. Part 2 reinterprets the two numbers as 1-based positions with an XOR check; the work is parsing plus simple character counting.

days.title: Toboggan Trajectory
days.essence: Walk a fixed slope across a 2D map that repeats horizontally (modular x), counting trees hit. Part 2 multiplies tree counts over several slopes; the trick is the horizontal wrap-around.

days.title: Passport Processing
days.essence: Parse whitespace/newline-separated key:value passport batches and check required fields. Part 2 adds a long list of per-field validation rules (year ranges, height units, hex colour, eye colour, pid format) — fiddly bookkeeping rather than a clever trick.

days.title: Binary Boarding
days.essence: Binary space partitioning of seats is just binary: F/B and L/R map to 0/1, so each boarding pass decodes to a seat-ID integer. Part 2 finds the single missing ID whose neighbours are present.

days.title: Custom Customs
days.essence: Split blank-line-separated groups of people and count answers. Part 1 is the union of letters per group, part 2 the intersection; elementary set logic over grouped input.

days.title: Handy Haversacks
days.essence: Bag-containment rules form a directed weighted graph. Part 1 asks which bags can eventually reach 'shiny gold' (reachability); part 2 recursively counts the total bags contained (a weighted sum over the DAG, memoized in the solution).

days.title: Handheld Halting
days.essence: Implement a tiny acc/jmp/nop interpreter and detect the infinite loop via visited instructions. Part 2 flips a single jmp/nop so the program terminates, found by trying each candidate.

days.title: Encoding Error
days.essence: Find the first number that is not a sum of two values in the preceding 25-number window (a windowed two-sum). Part 2 then locates a contiguous run summing to that number and adds its min and max.

days.title: Adapter Array
days.essence: Part 1 sorts the adapters and counts 1- and 3-jolt gaps. Part 2 counts all valid arrangements — astronomically many, so naive enumeration explodes and a memoized count of paths to each adapter is required.

days.title: Seating System
days.essence: Game-of-Life seating: empty/occupied seats flip simultaneously based on occupied neighbours until stable. Part 2 changes 'neighbour' from adjacent cells to the first visible seat along each of the eight lines of sight.

days.title: Rain Risk
days.essence: Follow navigation instructions and report Manhattan distance from origin. Part 1 turns the ship's heading directly; part 2 rotates a relative waypoint around the ship (the 90-degree vector rotations are where it gets tricky).

days.title: Shuttle Search
days.essence: Part 1 is a trivial modulo to find the earliest bus. Part 2 asks for the timestamp where every bus departs at its list offset — the classic Chinese Remainder Theorem problem, infeasible by brute force.

days.title: Docking Data
days.essence: Apply a 36-bit mask of 0/1/X to values written to memory. Part 1 overwrites value bits; part 2 makes X bits 'floating' in the address, expanding each write to 2^(#X) addresses (a combinatorial enumeration).

days.title: Rambunctious Recitation
days.essence: The Van Eck-style memory game: each turn speaks the gap since the previous occurrence of the last number. The key is caching each number's last-seen turn; part 2 runs to 30,000,000 turns, forcing an efficient O(n) map rather than rescanning.

days.title: Ticket Translation
days.essence: Part 1 sums values valid for no field rule. Part 2 deduces which column is which field by intersecting candidate fields per column and repeatedly eliminating singletons — a constraint-propagation matching problem.

days.title: Conway Cubes
days.essence: Conway's Game of Life lifted into 3D (then 4D in part 2): cubes toggle from their 26 (or 80) neighbours over six cycles. The challenge is bookkeeping an unbounded multi-dimensional grid via a sparse active-cell set.

days.title: Operation Order
days.essence: Evaluate arithmetic expressions with non-standard precedence and parentheses. Part 1 makes + and * equal (left-to-right); part 2 gives + higher precedence than *, so the evaluator must respect the new rules while recursing into bracketed sub-expressions.

days.title: Monster Messages
days.essence: Match messages against a recursive grammar of rules with alternation, counting strings that fully satisfy rule 0. Part 2 introduces self-referential (looping) rules 8 and 11, turning it into proper recursive context-free-grammar matching.

days.title: Jurassic Jigsaw
days.essence: Reassemble image tiles by matching edges under all rotations/flips (part 1 just multiplies the four corner IDs). Part 2 stitches the full image, strips borders, and searches every orientation for sea-monster patterns to find roughness — notoriously heavy implementation.

days.title: Allergen Assessment
days.essence: Each allergen must be one ingredient appearing in every food that lists it; intersecting candidate sets and eliminating singletons pins each allergen down. Part 1 counts allergen-free ingredients, part 2 outputs the sorted dangerous list.

days.title: Crab Combat
days.essence: Part 1 is a straight queue simulation of the card game War. Part 2 (Recursive Combat) spawns recursive sub-games from the drawn cards and must memoize seen deck states within a game to prevent infinite loops.

days.title: Crab Cups
days.essence: Simulate cup moves around a circle, splicing three cups after a destination. Part 1's 100 moves allow a naive list, but part 2's one million cups and ten million moves demand a next-pointer (linked-list) array for O(1) splices.

days.title: Lobby Layout
days.essence: Part 1 walks hex-grid direction strings (e/se/sw/w/nw/ne) to flip tiles, using axial coordinates to land on the right cell. Part 2 runs a Game of Life on that hexagonal grid for 100 days.

days.title: Combo Breaker
days.essence: A Diffie-Hellman key exchange: brute-force the discrete logarithm (loop size) of one public key by transforming subject 7 mod 20201227, then apply it to the other key via modular exponentiation to recover the shared key. The single-star finale.
